summary: Ayurvedic health assessment app with Motoko backend on Internet Computer. Implements rule-based pattern recognition for scoring 40-question assessment across 4 sections (Sleep/Hydration, Gut/Metabolism, Movement/Circulation, Mind/Emotional Balance). Provides personalized Ayurvedic solution tips based on detected patterns and medical history (BP, Sugar).
architectural_notes: Backend structure: main.mo (canister + API), types.mo (data models), scoring.mo (0-160 scoring), storage.mo (stable HashMap), analysis.mo (pattern recognition)
Pattern recognition is 100% rule-based and deterministic - no AI models used
Medical history keywords: 'BP' and 'Sugar' used for condition matching
Score ranges: 0-20 Alert, 21-30 Needs Work, 31-40 Good per section (4 sections × 40 = 160 total)
Test mode is internal-only for developer testing

existing_features.title: Create a frontend Test Mode page accessible only for internal developer testing that displays all three test profiles with their complete analysis results including section scores, detected patterns, solution tips, and medical history effects in a visual format matching the existing results page design.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create internal test mode page with 3 test profiles (Healthy, Moderate with BP, Serious with Sugar+BP) using automatic realistic score patterns
Implement backend test functions with console logging for pattern detection verification

Create backend test functions in main.mo that generate three test profiles: (1) Healthy User with all section scores in 30-40 range and no medical history, (2) Moderate Issues user with scores in 21-30 range and 'BP' medical history, (3) Serious Concerns user with scores in 0-20 range and 'Sugar, BP' medical history. Each profile must have realistic score patterns automatically generated across all 40 questions based on the profile type.

Add backend verification functions that process each test profile through the pattern analysis engine and log complete results to console, including detected low-scoring questions, identified patterns, generated solution tips, and medical history integration effects.
